interactions.summary: Jiang says secret societies or supranational organizations sit above nation-states, accelerate geopolitical events for profit, and may use imperial collapse to force end-times tribulation and a messianic one-world government.
The host asks whether religious calling in the military makes the war a serious miscalculation, likely to become protracted, and potentially nuclear.

Jiang says the eschatological script could make the war never end: U.S. ground troops lose to Iran, CENTCOM collapses, America retreats, Israel dominates the region, Iran rebuilds stronger, and Al-Aqsa becomes a next script point.

speaker_notes.note: The host's war-protraction question is performed and integrates religious-calling, miscalculation, and nuclear risk.

claims.claim: Jiang says nation-states are not the real constituent bodies of world events; supranational secret societies or organizations manipulate or accelerate geopolitics from above.

Jiang says these groups may seek to use imperial collapse to usher in tribulation, mass death, a messianic age, and one-world government, framing acceleration as mercy compared with slow imperial death.
claims.temporal_scope: Speculative eschatological account in this interview.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts that if America sends ground troops into Iran, it will lose badly, CENTCOM will collapse, America will retreat from the Middle East, and Iran will eventually rebuild stronger.

signature_moments.moment: The end-times accelerators see empire as a suffering creature to be shot so a new world can be born.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the world is dying. Just shoot it to death. So a new world can be born
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the chilling moral logic Jiang attributes to the eschatological faction.

signature_moments.moment: The war may not be designed to end; it may be a sequence meant to keep unfolding.
signature_moments.source_phrase: it is possible the stupid war never ends
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It states the protraction risk that drives the host's question.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The end-times peace and one-world government that Jiang says some actors may try to force through tribulation.
The years or decades of war, suffering, collapse, and death Jiang places before the proposed messianic age.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang contrasts a natural American imperial decline that could take about 20 years with accelerated collapse under the eschatological theory.

uncertainty_notes.note: Several named organizations are ASR-corrupted; treat the list cautiously and avoid over-normalizing without audio review.
Jiang marks this as a possibility, not a known fact; public prose should preserve that modal status.
